stories.title: Israel strikes Iran's oil facilities as crude tops $90
stories.description: Saturday's attacks marked the first time the air campaign targeted Iran's energy infrastructure, as Strait of Hormuz shipping grinds to a halt.

stories.title: Renewed MH370 search yields no findings 12 years after plane vanished
stories.description: Malaysia's AAIB confirmed the 28-day deep-sea search by Ocean Infinity covered over 7,500 sq km of seabed but found no trace of the aircraft.

stories.title: Health experts urge caution as daylight saving time returns Sunday
stories.description: Research links the spring time change to higher rates of heart attacks, strokes, fatal car crashes, and workplace injuries in the days that follow.

stories.title: Iranian drone damages Bahrain desalination plant as war enters ninth day
stories.description: Bahrain's interior ministry says the strike marks the first time a Gulf state has reported Iranian damage to critical water infrastructure in the conflict.

stories.title: Judge voids Kari Lake's Voice of America cuts, rules her tenure unlawful
stories.description: A federal judge declared all actions Lake took as acting USAGM CEO "null and void," finding her appointment violated the Federal Vacancies Reform Act.

stories.title: Israel targets Tehran oil sites and Mehrabad airport in new strikes
stories.description: Netanyahu vowed to continue attacks with "full force," promising "many surprises" as the conflict's death toll surpasses 1,500.

stories.title: Ukraine says it halted Russia's advance toward Zaporizhzhia in three-month operation
stories.description: HUR claims its special forces killed or wounded over 300 Russian soldiers and captured 39 more while stabilizing defensive lines in the south.

stories.title: Trump formally recognizes Venezuela's Rodriguez government
stories.description: The announcement came at the Shield of the Americas Summit in Florida, alongside a new deal for Venezuelan gold and mineral exports to the U.S.

stories.title: Dubai airport resumes flights after Iranian drone explodes near terminal
stories.description: Emirates temporarily halted all operations Saturday after a drone detonated near Concourse A, the second such incident in a week.

stories.title: Guinea dissolves 40 political parties, including top opposition groups
stories.description: The late-night decree strips parties of legal status and seizes their assets less than two months after former junta leader Doumbouya's inauguration.

stories.title: Trump doesn't rule out ground troops in Iran
stories.description: Speaking aboard Air Force One after honoring six fallen soldiers at Dover, the president said deploying troops to secure enriched uranium "is something we could consider later."

stories.title: Turkey weighs deploying F-16 jets to Northern Cyprus
stories.description: The move follows a wave of European military deployments to the divided island after a drone struck Britain's RAF Akrotiri base last week.

stories.title: Protests erupt across Havana after days without power
stories.description: Cubans banged pots and shouted slogans in darkened streets Friday night after enduring up to 80 hours without electricity amid a deepening energy crisis.

stories.title: Trump honors six US troops killed in Kuwait at Dover ceremony
stories.description: The ceremony marked the first dignified transfer since the U.S. and Israel launched strikes on Iran on Feb. 28, confronting Trump with costs of a war he initiated.

stories.title: UAE president declares 'we are no easy prey' as Iranian strikes enter second week
stories.description: Mohammed bin Zayed's first public comments since the attacks began came during a hospital visit to wounded civilians in Abu Dhabi.

stories.title: Balen Shah's party heads for landslide in Nepal's post-protest election
stories.description: The rapper-turned-politician defeated ex-PM KP Sharma Oli and his RSP leads in over 100 constituencies, shattering traditional party dominance.

stories.title: DOGE used ChatGPT to flag humanities grants for cancellation, court filings reveal
stories.description: Discovery documents show the efficiency office fed grant descriptions into the AI chatbot to label projects as "DEI," bypassing NEH staff.

stories.title: US deploys third aircraft carrier USS George HW Bush to Middle East
stories.description: The deployment would create the largest American naval aviation presence in the Middle East since the 2003 invasion of Iraq.

stories.title: Chinese study on US missile defense gaps validated by Iran war, analysts say
stories.description: Iran's hypersonic missiles have repeatedly penetrated THAAD and other advanced US defense systems since the conflict began Feb. 28.

stories.title: CBP says it needs 45 days to build $166B tariff refund system
stories.description: More than 330,000 importers await reimbursement after the Supreme Court struck down Trump's emergency tariffs last month.

stories.title: US suspects Chinese hackers breached FBI wiretap network
stories.description: The Wall Street Journal reported Friday that hackers linked to China's government infiltrated an FBI system holding domestic surveillance data.

stories.title: US clears $152M emergency arms sale to Israel, bypassing Congress
stories.description: Secretary of State Rubio invoked emergency powers to expedite 12,000 bomb bodies to Israel as the U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran intensifies.

stories.title: White House dismisses Russia sharing intel with Iran to target US troops
stories.description: Press Secretary Leavitt said reports of Russian intelligence aid to Iran "frankly" don't matter, drawing sharp bipartisan criticism.

stories.title: US military carries out lethal strikes in Ecuador
stories.description: The Pentagon confirmed Friday's operations targeted a narco-terrorist supply complex, escalating from an advisory role that began days earlier.

stories.title: Cuba shuts Quito embassy after Ecuador expels diplomats
stories.description: The closure came after a 48-hour ultimatum expired, marking one of Latin America's sharpest diplomatic ruptures in years.

stories.title: Trump threatens expanded strikes on Iran as war enters second week
stories.description: Iran's president rejected demands for unconditional surrender while apologizing to Gulf neighbors for Iranian strikes as the conflict enters its eighth day.

stories.title: Iran warns Europe of retaliation if nations join US-Israeli strikes
stories.description: Deputy Foreign Minister Takht-Ravanchi issued the threat as France, Britain, and Italy accelerate military deployments across the Mediterranean and Gulf.

stories.title: UN chief warns Iran conflict could spiral out of control
stories.description: Guterres condemned "unlawful attacks" across the Middle East and called for immediate diplomatic negotiations as the war enters its second week.

stories.title: Iran's Guards dare US Navy to escort tankers through Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: An IRGC spokesperson said Iran "strongly welcomes" and awaits the arrival of American forces, warning the U.S. to recall past tanker attacks.

stories.title: Pakistani man convicted in Iran-backed plot to kill Trump, US politicians
stories.description: A federal jury in Brooklyn found Asif Merchant guilty of conspiring to assassinate U.S. politicians in a scheme prosecutors tied to Iran's IRGC.

stories.title: Bessent says US may ease more Russia oil sanctions
stories.description: The Treasury secretary's comments came a day after Washington granted India a 30-day waiver to buy sanctioned Russian crude stranded at sea.

stories.title: Pentagon sends Ukraine-tested Merops anti-drone system to Middle East
stories.description: Officials admit U.S. forces lack adequate defenses against Iranian drone waves targeting personnel in the Gulf region.

stories.title: US, Ukraine discuss free-trade pact and recovery fund, minister says
stories.description: Ukraine's economy minister said negotiators are working on a trade agreement and sovereign fund as part of a broader postwar prosperity package.

stories.title: Israel bombing western Iran to back Kurdish offensive
stories.description: Sources say thousands of Kurdish fighters are massing on the Iraqi border, preparing to seize Iranian towns within a week.

stories.title: Judge skeptical of Pentagon rules barring unapproved reporting by journalists
stories.description: U.S. District Judge Paul Friedman sharply questioned the government's defense of press restrictions, telling the courtroom "asking a question is not criminal."

stories.title: Lewandowski leaves DHS after Noem's firing as secretary
stories.description: Trump adviser Corey Lewandowski wielded outsized power as Noem's unofficial chief of staff despite holding unpaid, part-time status.

stories.title: US push for Venezuela mining access threatens environmental crisis
stories.description: The Interior secretary met with Venezuela's acting president and said the government offered security guarantees for companies entering mineral-rich regions.

stories.title: Newly revealed DEA probe linked Epstein to ketamine trade
stories.description: A newly detailed investigation called "Operation Chain Reaction" tracked roughly $50 million in suspicious wire transfers tied to drug trafficking and money laundering.

stories.title: White House expects Iran operation to take 4 to 6 weeks
stories.description: Spokeswoman Karoline Leavitt said the U.S. has sunk over 30 Iranian vessels and cut retaliatory missile strikes by 90% in seven days.

stories.title: US retail sales fell in January, extending trend
stories.description: Consumer spending dropped broadly, with department stores, personal care outlets, and gas stations leading the declines.

stories.title: Kuwait cuts oil output as storage fills amid Hormuz blockade
stories.description: The OPEC founding member may limit production to domestic-only levels within days as the Gulf storage crisis spreads from Iraq.

stories.title: Trump says defense firms agreed to quadruple advanced weapons production
stories.description: The announcement followed a White House meeting with six major contractors as the U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran enters its second week.

stories.title: UK arrests four men suspected of spying on Jewish sites for Iran
stories.description: Counter-terrorism police detained one Iranian national and three dual British-Iranian citizens in a pre-dawn operation across London.

stories.title: US economy sheds 92,000 jobs in February, defying forecasts
stories.description: The unemployment rate rose to 4.4% as employers cut payrolls instead of adding the 59,000 jobs economists had expected.

stories.title: Iceland sets Aug. 29 referendum on resuming EU talks
stories.description: The government unanimously agreed to fast-track the vote, originally pledged for 2027, amid security concerns tied to Trump's Greenland threats.

stories.title: Iran reports 1,332 civilian deaths in UN letter on sixth day of war
stories.description: Tehran's UN envoy urged the Security Council to take "urgent, collective measures" to halt US-Israeli strikes on Iranian territory.

stories.title: Axel Springer agrees to buy UK's Telegraph for £575M
stories.description: The German media giant's deal upends the Daily Mail owner's planned acquisition and places the 168-year-old conservative paper under foreign ownership.

stories.title: European stocks rise as oil eases after sharpest weekly surge since 2022
stories.description: The Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed to commercial shipping as the U.S.-Iran conflict disrupts nearly 20% of global oil and gas flows.

stories.title: Jimmy Lai won't appeal 20-year Hong Kong sentence
stories.description: The 78-year-old Apple Daily founder's legal team confirmed the decision without explanation, closing one of Hong Kong's most prominent national security cases.

stories.title: Texas Rep. Tony Gonzales ends reelection bid amid ethics probe
stories.description: The Texas Republican withdrew hours after House Speaker Mike Johnson and GOP leadership publicly called on him to end his campaign.

stories.title: SoftBank seeks record $40B loan to fund OpenAI bet
stories.description: The Japanese conglomerate is in talks with JPMorgan and three other banks for a 12-month bridge loan, according to Bloomberg.

stories.title: U.S. probe points to likely American role in Iran school strike
stories.description: A preliminary U.S. military investigation found American forces were likely behind a strike on a girls' school that killed dozens of children, Reuters reports.

stories.title: Noem bypassed bidding on $100M ICE contracts, NBC reports
stories.description: The outgoing DHS secretary handpicked the same politically connected firms already under scrutiny for a separate $220 million ad campaign.

stories.title: UAE weighs freezing billions in Iranian assets
stories.description: The move would target Tehran's financial networks in Dubai as Iranian missile and drone strikes on the Gulf state enter a second week.

stories.title: US reports 90 percent drop in Iranian ballistic missile attacks
stories.description: CENTCOM's commander said B-2 bombers struck nearly 200 targets deep inside Iran in the past 72 hours as offensive operations shift to dismantling Tehran's production capacity.

stories.title: Paxton offers to exit Texas Senate runoff if voter ID bill passes
stories.description: After Trump warned that Paxton's refusal to drop out could cost him the endorsement, the attorney general set conditions for a potential withdrawal.

stories.title: Senate blocks DHS funding for third time as shutdown drags on
stories.description: Republicans warn the partial shutdown poses national security risks amid the U.S. military conflict with Iran, but Democrats demand immigration enforcement reforms.

stories.title: Iraq's first lady tells US to 'leave the Kurds alone'
stories.description: Shanaz Ibrahim Ahmed warned against enlisting Kurdish fighters as proxies in the war against Iran, saying "we are not guns for hire."

stories.title: DOJ releases withheld Epstein files tied to Trump allegations
stories.description: The department said three FBI interview memos were "incorrectly coded as duplicative" and left out of the public archive.

stories.title: Warren introduces bill to block US ammunition sales linked to Mexican cartels
stories.description: The legislation targets a Missouri plant whose rounds have been traced to Mexican cartels and at least a dozen U.S. mass shootings.

stories.title: US, EU issue joint call to end Congo ceasefire violations
stories.description: The statement follows Washington's sanctions on Rwanda's military days earlier for backing M23 rebels in eastern DRC.

stories.title: Appeals court backs Trump's power to halt refugee program
stories.description: The Ninth Circuit ruled the president can indefinitely suspend refugee admissions but must keep funding services for refugees already in the U.S.

stories.title: Iran says it's ready for US ground invasion, rejects ceasefire
stories.description: Foreign Minister Araghchi told NBC News that Iran is "not requesting ceasefire" and is confident it can confront American ground forces.

stories.title: Zelenskyy says Ukraine-Russia peace talks on hold amid Iran war
stories.description: The Ukrainian president said US-brokered negotiations were postponed indefinitely after the Iran conflict disrupted diplomatic plans and closed UAE airspace.

stories.title: House rejects bid to halt Trump's Iran strikes
stories.description: The vote came a day after the Senate defeated a similar measure, signaling broad Republican support for the U.S.-Israeli military campaign.

stories.title: Iranian drones strike Azerbaijan airport as war spreads to South Caucasus
stories.description: Azerbaijan vowed a response after drones launched from Iran hit Nakhchivan International Airport, injuring two civilians and damaging the terminal.

stories.title: ECB warns Iran war's economic fallout poses risk to euro zone banks
stories.description: A senior ECB supervisor said direct bank exposure to the conflict is minimal, but rising energy prices could squeeze borrowers and reignite inflation.

stories.title: Federal panel delays Trump ballroom vote after public outcry
stories.description: The National Capital Planning Commission rescheduled its final vote to April 2 after receiving over 35,000 mostly negative written comments.

stories.title: Gold pulls back from record highs as Iran war enters second week
stories.description: After surging past $5,400 per ounce, gold prices have retreated amid profit-taking but remain elevated as the Middle East conflict widens.

stories.title: Bulk carrier signals 'China owner' to cross Hormuz blockade
stories.description: The Iron Maiden changed its destination tag to broadcast Chinese ownership as it transited the war-zone strait early Thursday.

stories.title: Trump launches 'Shield of the Americas' anti-cartel initiative
stories.description: Noem will leave DHS to lead the new Western Hemisphere security effort as 17 nations signed a joint declaration at a first-ever counter-cartel conference.

stories.title: Sri Lanka recovers 87 bodies from Iranian warship sunk by US submarine
stories.description: The IRIS Dena was hit by a single torpedo off Sri Lanka's coast, marking the first U.S. submarine kill since World War II.

stories.title: Alibaba forms AI task force after Qwen chief's abrupt exit
stories.description: CEO Eddie Wu will lead the effort to accelerate model development after three senior Qwen executives departed in 2026.

stories.title: Trump calls Israeli President Herzog a 'disgrace,' demands Netanyahu pardon
stories.description: The U.S. president said he wants Netanyahu focused on the Iran war, not his corruption trial, and vowed not to meet Herzog until a pardon is granted.

stories.title: Russian oil tankers reroute to India as Hormuz crisis chokes supply
stories.description: As the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed following U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran, India is turning back to Russian crude it had been avoiding under American pressure.

stories.title: Bloomberg: Epstein used Hoffman link to reach Big Tech leaders
stories.description: Documents released by the DOJ reveal the LinkedIn co-founder's ties to Epstein went far beyond the fundraising relationship he has claimed.

stories.title: 24 state AGs sue to block Trump's replacement tariffs
stories.description: New York Attorney General Letitia James leads a coalition challenging the legality of tariffs imposed under a never-before-used 1974 trade law.

stories.title: Democrats flip 9th state seat since Trump took office
stories.description: Democrat Alex Holladay won an Arkansas House special election Tuesday, extending a streak in which Republicans have flipped zero Democratic-held seats.

stories.title: US and Venezuela probe secret Maduro-era oil deals
stories.description: Dozens of confidential contracts let investors pump Venezuelan crude while hiding their identities to evade U.S. sanctions, according to Bloomberg.

stories.title: China sets slowest defense budget growth since 2022 at 7%
stories.description: The official military budget rises to 1.91 trillion yuan ($277 billion), even as the U.S. and allies accelerate their own spending.

stories.title: Appeals court blocks Musk deposition in USAID lawsuit
stories.description: A federal appeals court ruled 2-1 that Musk does not have to testify under oath about his role in dismantling the foreign aid agency while leading DOGE.

stories.title: Venezuela to reform mining law after Burgum visit
stories.description: Acting President Rodriguez said the legislation, which would open gold, diamonds and rare earths to foreign companies, will be submitted in days.

stories.title: Noem defends record in two days of heated Hill testimony
stories.description: The DHS secretary faced bipartisan criticism over Minneapolis shootings, FEMA delays, and a GOP senator's public call for her resignation.

stories.title: Johnson says US 'not at war' with Iran, contradicting Trump
stories.description: The House speaker's claim clashes with the president's and defense secretary's own use of the word "war" to describe Operation Epic Fury.

stories.title: Iran conflict threatens to divert US air defense missiles from Ukraine
stories.description: Analysts warn that roughly 600 PAC-3 interceptors produced annually cannot meet demand from both Gulf allies and Ukraine.

stories.title: Rubio pledges full US support to Turkey after NATO downs Iranian missile
stories.description: NATO intercepted a ballistic missile from Iran heading toward Turkish airspace, the first such defense of a member state since the conflict erupted.

stories.title: Tech giants sign Trump pledge to fund their own data center power
stories.description: Seven major tech companies agreed to build or buy all electricity needed for AI data centers and prevent costs from reaching consumers.

stories.title: Iraq suffers nationwide blackout as war disrupts gas supplies
stories.description: A fuel shortage at a Basra power plant caused a cascading grid failure, as the U.S.-Israel-Iran conflict chokes Iraq's Iranian gas imports.

stories.title: House panel subpoenas AG Bondi over Epstein files
stories.description: Five Republicans joined all Democrats on the Oversight Committee in a 24-19 vote compelling Bondi to testify on withheld Epstein documents.

stories.title: DOJ proposes rule to let Bondi block state bar ethics probes into its lawyers
stories.description: The regulation, published Wednesday in the Federal Register, would let the attorney general suspend state-level investigations while DOJ conducts its own review.

stories.title: New study finds sea levels far higher than assumed, raising flood risk for millions
stories.description: Lidar satellite data reveals coastal lands are much lower than radar-based models suggested, tripling the population estimated to be at risk.

stories.title: Bondi lets DOJ appointees attend partisan events again
stories.description: The attorney general revoked Biden-era rules that barred political appointees from campaign rallies, fundraisers, and other partisan activities.

stories.title: European stocks post biggest gain since May on Iran diplomacy hopes
stories.description: Markets rallied Wednesday after reports that Iran made indirect contact with the U.S. to negotiate an end to the conflict.

stories.title: Talarico wins Texas Democratic Senate nod amid voting chaos
stories.description: Hundreds of Dallas voters were turned away at polls after a GOP-driven shift to precinct-specific voting sparked court battles over extended hours.

stories.title: Pentagon says it killed Iranian commander behind Trump assassination plot
stories.description: The announcement came on day five of Operation Epic Fury, the U.S.-Israeli campaign that has already killed Iran's supreme leader.

stories.title: SpaceX launches 600th Starlink satellite of 2026
stories.description: The Falcon 9 mission from Cape Canaveral carried 29 satellites, while SpaceX eyes commercial Starship operations as soon as next year.

stories.title: Cooper, Whatley win NC Senate primary showdown
stories.description: The former Democratic governor and Trump-backed ex-RNC co-chair will face off in a race that could help decide Senate control.

stories.title: Chinese projects in Iran face uncertainty amid US-Israeli strikes
stories.description: Reuters found state-backed Chinese firms were pursuing energy, construction, and trade projects in Iran just weeks before the military campaign began.

stories.title: Kuwaiti F/A-18 pilot fired 3 missiles that downed U.S. F-15s, reports say
stories.description: Initial reports point to a single Kuwaiti fighter jet, not ground-based air defenses, as the source of the friendly fire incident that destroyed three American warplanes.

stories.title: Hinojosa wins Texas Democratic primary, will face Abbott in November
stories.description: The state representative captured over 60% of the vote, setting up what polls suggest could be Texas' most competitive governor's race in years.

stories.title: Israel vows to assassinate Khamenei's successor as son Mojtaba reportedly chosen
stories.description: Iran's Assembly of Experts named Mojtaba Khamenei as supreme leader under IRGC pressure, days after his father was killed in a U.S.-Israeli strike.

stories.title: House panel seeks testimony from Gates, Black in Epstein probe
stories.description: Commerce Secretary Lutnick also agreed to testify voluntarily, avoiding a potential subpoena over his ties to Jeffrey Epstein.

stories.title: US readying draft indictment of Venezuela's interim leader Rodriguez
stories.description: Federal prosecutors are allegedly preparing corruption and money laundering charges as leverage over Venezuela's interim president, according to Reuters.

stories.title: Iran warns advanced weapons not yet deployed in conflict
stories.description: Tehran's Defense Ministry claims it has reserved its most powerful systems, rejecting Trump's assertion that Iran's military has been neutralized.

stories.title: Ship hit near UAE's Fujairah as Gulf attacks escalate
stories.description: The latest strike is part of a wave of attacks on commercial shipping since U.S.-Israeli operations against Iran began on Feb. 28.

stories.title: Poll finds half of Americans now support abolishing ICE
stories.description: Support hit 50% for the first time in YouGov polling, with opposition falling to an all-time low of 39%, amid fallout from deadly shootings.

stories.title: Merz meets Trump as Germany's military buildup reshapes Europe
stories.description: Germany's chancellor visited the White House as Berlin's plan to more than double defense spending draws both applause and anxiety from European allies.

stories.title: Survey finds one in three European women face violence
stories.description: A sweeping new survey of more than 114,000 women across all 27 EU member states found that most cases of violence go unreported.

stories.title: Syria deploys thousands of troops to Lebanon border
stories.description: The buildup, which began in February and accelerated recently, aims to block Hezbollah infiltration, according to Reuters.

stories.title: Dr. Oz opens fraud probe into New York's Medicaid program
stories.description: The CMS administrator sent Gov. Hochul 50 questions about cost controls and provider oversight in the state's $124 billion program.

stories.title: Sudan accuses Ethiopia of drone attacks in its civil war
stories.description: Khartoum says drones launched from Ethiopian territory struck targets inside Sudan, the first time it has directly implicated its neighbor in the conflict.

stories.title: Israel says it destroyed secret Iranian nuclear site near Tehran
stories.description: The IDF said it struck a covert underground facility where scientists were developing nuclear weapons components, the first such site revealed since June 2025.

stories.title: Lutnick volunteers to testify on Epstein ties
stories.description: The commerce secretary's offer comes as lawmakers were preparing a bipartisan subpoena to compel his appearance before the House Oversight Committee.

stories.title: Ukraine refuses EU requests to inspect damaged Druzhba pipeline
stories.description: Kyiv denied access to the EU ambassador and top EU leaders, citing security concerns, as Hungary leverages the dispute to block a €90 billion loan.

stories.title: FCC chair says Paramount-WBD merger will be approved 'quickly'
stories.description: FCC Chairman Brendan Carr told CNBC the deal is "cleaner" than Netflix's earlier bid, signaling a smoother regulatory path for the $110 billion acquisition.

stories.title: DOJ reverses itself, seeks to revive Trump law firm cases
stories.description: Hours after moving to drop appeals of rulings that struck down Trump's executive orders targeting four law firms, the Justice Department reversed course.

stories.title: IAEA confirms damage at Iran's Natanz nuclear site
stories.description: The U.N. nuclear watchdog said satellite imagery shows entrance buildings were hit but no radiological consequences are expected.

stories.title: Supreme Court lets California schools tell parents about child's gender identity
stories.description: The 6-3 ruling reinstates a federal injunction blocking state laws that limited schools from disclosing students' transgender status to parents.

stories.title: Noem refuses to retract 'terrorist' label for slain citizens
stories.description: At a Senate hearing, the DHS secretary denied calling Alex Pretti a "domestic terrorist," saying she described the incident, not the person.

stories.title: Ukraine says it recaptured more land than Russia seized in February
stories.description: Kyiv's top general called it the first month Ukraine outpaced Russia in territorial gains since the 2024 Kursk incursion.

stories.title: Supreme Court blocks redrawing of NYC's only GOP district
stories.description: The ruling preserves Rep. Nicole Malliotakis's Staten Island-based seat for 2026, halting a state court order that found the map dilutes minority votes.

stories.title: Russia's Medvedev warns of World War III over US strikes on Iran
stories.description: The deputy chairman of Russia's Security Council called Trump's regime-change campaign "a grave mistake" that has put all Americans at risk.

stories.title: Bolivia raids homes to recover cash looted from deadly plane crash
stories.description: Security forces searched at least 22 homes near El Alto, where a military plane carrying $62 million in new banknotes crashed last week, killing 22.

stories.title: Judge blocks ICE visit restrictions for all of Congress
stories.description: A federal judge struck down DHS's seven-day notice requirement for the third time, now extending the ruling to protect all lawmakers, not just the 13 who sued.

stories.title: Judge rules Trump bid to kill NYC congestion pricing unlawful
stories.description: U.S. District Judge Lewis Liman found the Transportation Department's 2025 move to terminate Manhattan's first-in-the-nation tolling program exceeded its authority.

stories.title: House panel releases Clinton's Epstein deposition videos
stories.description: More than nine hours of footage show Bill and Hillary Clinton answering questions about Jeffrey Epstein after months of legal battles.

stories.title: Rubio says Israel's strike plan triggered US attack on Iran
stories.description: The secretary of state said the U.S. struck Iran preemptively after learning Israel would attack, fearing Tehran's automatic retaliation against American forces.

stories.title: Treasury ends Anthropic AI use as agencies comply with Trump ban
stories.description: Federal housing agencies also cut ties with the AI company, which refused to drop safety guardrails on surveillance and autonomous weapons for the Pentagon.

stories.title: Sanders and Khanna introduce 5% wealth tax on billionaires
stories.description: The bill would tax America's 938 billionaires to fund $3,000 direct payments to most households and expand social programs.

stories.title: Navy intel chief warns China could strike US from its own waters by 2030s
stories.description: New submarines armed with advanced ballistic missiles will be able to target large portions of the U.S. from Chinese coastal waters, Rear Adm. Brookes told Congress.

stories.title: Rubio says US ground troops not needed in Iran
stories.description: Secretary of State Rubio told lawmakers the campaign's goals can be met through air and naval power, but Trump hasn't ruled out ground forces.

top.description: More than 330,000 importers await reimbursement after the Supreme Court struck down Trump's emergency tariffs last month.

top.title: US suspects Chinese hackers breached FBI wiretap network
top.description: The Wall Street Journal reported Friday that hackers linked to China's government infiltrated an FBI system holding domestic surveillance data.

top.title: US clears $152M emergency arms sale to Israel, bypassing Congress
top.description: Secretary of State Rubio invoked emergency powers to expedite 12,000 bomb bodies to Israel as the U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran intensifies.

top.title: White House dismisses Russia sharing intel with Iran to target US troops
top.description: Press Secretary Leavitt said reports of Russian intelligence aid to Iran "frankly" don't matter, drawing sharp bipartisan criticism.

top.title: US military carries out lethal strikes in Ecuador
top.description: The Pentagon confirmed Friday's operations targeted a narco-terrorist supply complex, escalating from an advisory role that began days earlier.

top.title: Cuba shuts Quito embassy after Ecuador expels diplomats
top.description: The closure came after a 48-hour ultimatum expired, marking one of Latin America's sharpest diplomatic ruptures in years.

top.title: Trump threatens expanded strikes on Iran as war enters second week
top.description: Iran's president rejected demands for unconditional surrender while apologizing to Gulf neighbors for Iranian strikes as the conflict enters its eighth day.

top.title: Iran warns Europe of retaliation if nations join US-Israeli strikes
top.description: Deputy Foreign Minister Takht-Ravanchi issued the threat as France, Britain, and Italy accelerate military deployments across the Mediterranean and Gulf.

top.title: UN chief warns Iran conflict could spiral out of control
top.description: Guterres condemned "unlawful attacks" across the Middle East and called for immediate diplomatic negotiations as the war enters its second week.

top.title: Iran's Guards dare US Navy to escort tankers through Strait of Hormuz
top.description: An IRGC spokesperson said Iran "strongly welcomes" and awaits the arrival of American forces, warning the U.S. to recall past tanker attacks.

top.title: Pakistani man convicted in Iran-backed plot to kill Trump, US politicians
top.description: A federal jury in Brooklyn found Asif Merchant guilty of conspiring to assassinate U.S. politicians in a scheme prosecutors tied to Iran's IRGC.

top.title: Bessent says US may ease more Russia oil sanctions
top.description: The Treasury secretary's comments came a day after Washington granted India a 30-day waiver to buy sanctioned Russian crude stranded at sea.

top.title: Pentagon sends Ukraine-tested Merops anti-drone system to Middle East
top.description: Officials admit U.S. forces lack adequate defenses against Iranian drone waves targeting personnel in the Gulf region.

top.title: US, Ukraine discuss free-trade pact and recovery fund, minister says
top.description: Ukraine's economy minister said negotiators are working on a trade agreement and sovereign fund as part of a broader postwar prosperity package.

top.title: Israel bombing western Iran to back Kurdish offensive
top.description: Sources say thousands of Kurdish fighters are massing on the Iraqi border, preparing to seize Iranian towns within a week.

top.title: Judge skeptical of Pentagon rules barring unapproved reporting by journalists
top.description: U.S. District Judge Paul Friedman sharply questioned the government's defense of press restrictions, telling the courtroom "asking a question is not criminal."

top.title: Lewandowski leaves DHS after Noem's firing as secretary
top.description: Trump adviser Corey Lewandowski wielded outsized power as Noem's unofficial chief of staff despite holding unpaid, part-time status.

top.title: US push for Venezuela mining access threatens environmental crisis
top.description: The Interior secretary met with Venezuela's acting president and said the government offered security guarantees for companies entering mineral-rich regions.

top.title: Newly revealed DEA probe linked Epstein to ketamine trade
top.description: A newly detailed investigation called "Operation Chain Reaction" tracked roughly $50 million in suspicious wire transfers tied to drug trafficking and money laundering.

top.title: White House expects Iran operation to take 4 to 6 weeks
top.description: Spokeswoman Karoline Leavitt said the U.S. has sunk over 30 Iranian vessels and cut retaliatory missile strikes by 90% in seven days.

top.title: US retail sales fell in January, extending trend
top.description: Consumer spending dropped broadly, with department stores, personal care outlets, and gas stations leading the declines.

top.title: Kuwait cuts oil output as storage fills amid Hormuz blockade
top.description: The OPEC founding member may limit production to domestic-only levels within days as the Gulf storage crisis spreads from Iraq.

top.title: Trump says defense firms agreed to quadruple advanced weapons production
top.description: The announcement followed a White House meeting with six major contractors as the U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran enters its second week.

top.title: UK arrests four men suspected of spying on Jewish sites for Iran
top.description: Counter-terrorism police detained one Iranian national and three dual British-Iranian citizens in a pre-dawn operation across London.

top.title: US economy sheds 92,000 jobs in February, defying forecasts
top.description: The unemployment rate rose to 4.4% as employers cut payrolls instead of adding the 59,000 jobs economists had expected.

top.title: Iceland sets Aug. 29 referendum on resuming EU talks
top.description: The government unanimously agreed to fast-track the vote, originally pledged for 2027, amid security concerns tied to Trump's Greenland threats.

top.title: Iran reports 1,332 civilian deaths in UN letter on sixth day of war
top.description: Tehran's UN envoy urged the Security Council to take "urgent, collective measures" to halt US-Israeli strikes on Iranian territory.

top.title: Axel Springer agrees to buy UK's Telegraph for £575M
top.description: The German media giant's deal upends the Daily Mail owner's planned acquisition and places the 168-year-old conservative paper under foreign ownership.

top.title: European stocks rise as oil eases after sharpest weekly surge since 2022
top.description: The Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed to commercial shipping as the U.S.-Iran conflict disrupts nearly 20% of global oil and gas flows.

top.title: Jimmy Lai won't appeal 20-year Hong Kong sentence
top.description: The 78-year-old Apple Daily founder's legal team confirmed the decision without explanation, closing one of Hong Kong's most prominent national security cases.

top.title: Texas Rep. Tony Gonzales ends reelection bid amid ethics probe
top.description: The Texas Republican withdrew hours after House Speaker Mike Johnson and GOP leadership publicly called on him to end his campaign.

top.title: SoftBank seeks record $40B loan to fund OpenAI bet
top.description: The Japanese conglomerate is in talks with JPMorgan and three other banks for a 12-month bridge loan, according to Bloomberg.

top.title: U.S. probe points to likely American role in Iran school strike
top.description: A preliminary U.S. military investigation found American forces were likely behind a strike on a girls' school that killed dozens of children, Reuters reports.

top.title: Noem bypassed bidding on $100M ICE contracts, NBC reports
top.description: The outgoing DHS secretary handpicked the same politically connected firms already under scrutiny for a separate $220 million ad campaign.

top.title: UAE weighs freezing billions in Iranian assets
top.description: The move would target Tehran's financial networks in Dubai as Iranian missile and drone strikes on the Gulf state enter a second week.

top.title: US reports 90 percent drop in Iranian ballistic missile attacks
top.description: CENTCOM's commander said B-2 bombers struck nearly 200 targets deep inside Iran in the past 72 hours as offensive operations shift to dismantling Tehran's production capacity.

top.title: Paxton offers to exit Texas Senate runoff if voter ID bill passes
top.description: After Trump warned that Paxton's refusal to drop out could cost him the endorsement, the attorney general set conditions for a potential withdrawal.

top.title: Senate blocks DHS funding for third time as shutdown drags on
top.description: Republicans warn the partial shutdown poses national security risks amid the U.S. military conflict with Iran, but Democrats demand immigration enforcement reforms.

top.title: Iraq's first lady tells US to 'leave the Kurds alone'
top.description: Shanaz Ibrahim Ahmed warned against enlisting Kurdish fighters as proxies in the war against Iran, saying "we are not guns for hire."

top.title: DOJ releases withheld Epstein files tied to Trump allegations
top.description: The department said three FBI interview memos were "incorrectly coded as duplicative" and left out of the public archive.

top.title: Warren introduces bill to block US ammunition sales linked to Mexican cartels
top.description: The legislation targets a Missouri plant whose rounds have been traced to Mexican cartels and at least a dozen U.S. mass shootings.

top.title: US, EU issue joint call to end Congo ceasefire violations
top.description: The statement follows Washington's sanctions on Rwanda's military days earlier for backing M23 rebels in eastern DRC.

top.title: Appeals court backs Trump's power to halt refugee program
top.description: The Ninth Circuit ruled the president can indefinitely suspend refugee admissions but must keep funding services for refugees already in the U.S.

top.title: Iran says it's ready for US ground invasion, rejects ceasefire
top.description: Foreign Minister Araghchi told NBC News that Iran is "not requesting ceasefire" and is confident it can confront American ground forces.

top.title: Zelenskyy says Ukraine-Russia peace talks on hold amid Iran war
top.description: The Ukrainian president said US-brokered negotiations were postponed indefinitely after the Iran conflict disrupted diplomatic plans and closed UAE airspace.

top.title: House rejects bid to halt Trump's Iran strikes
top.description: The vote came a day after the Senate defeated a similar measure, signaling broad Republican support for the U.S.-Israeli military campaign.

top.title: Iranian drones strike Azerbaijan airport as war spreads to South Caucasus
top.description: Azerbaijan vowed a response after drones launched from Iran hit Nakhchivan International Airport, injuring two civilians and damaging the terminal.

top.title: ECB warns Iran war's economic fallout poses risk to euro zone banks
top.description: A senior ECB supervisor said direct bank exposure to the conflict is minimal, but rising energy prices could squeeze borrowers and reignite inflation.

top.title: Federal panel delays Trump ballroom vote after public outcry
top.description: The National Capital Planning Commission rescheduled its final vote to April 2 after receiving over 35,000 mostly negative written comments.

top.title: Gold pulls back from record highs as Iran war enters second week
top.description: After surging past $5,400 per ounce, gold prices have retreated amid profit-taking but remain elevated as the Middle East conflict widens.

top.title: Bulk carrier signals 'China owner' to cross Hormuz blockade
top.description: The Iron Maiden changed its destination tag to broadcast Chinese ownership as it transited the war-zone strait early Thursday.

top.title: Trump launches 'Shield of the Americas' anti-cartel initiative
top.description: Noem will leave DHS to lead the new Western Hemisphere security effort as 17 nations signed a joint declaration at a first-ever counter-cartel conference.

top.title: Sri Lanka recovers 87 bodies from Iranian warship sunk by US submarine
top.description: The IRIS Dena was hit by a single torpedo off Sri Lanka's coast, marking the first U.S. submarine kill since World War II.

top.title: Alibaba forms AI task force after Qwen chief's abrupt exit
top.description: CEO Eddie Wu will lead the effort to accelerate model development after three senior Qwen executives departed in 2026.

top.title: Trump calls Israeli President Herzog a 'disgrace,' demands Netanyahu pardon
top.description: The U.S. president said he wants Netanyahu focused on the Iran war, not his corruption trial, and vowed not to meet Herzog until a pardon is granted.

top.title: Russian oil tankers reroute to India as Hormuz crisis chokes supply
top.description: As the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed following U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran, India is turning back to Russian crude it had been avoiding under American pressure.

top.title: Bloomberg: Epstein used Hoffman link to reach Big Tech leaders
top.description: Documents released by the DOJ reveal the LinkedIn co-founder's ties to Epstein went far beyond the fundraising relationship he has claimed.

top.title: 24 state AGs sue to block Trump's replacement tariffs
top.description: New York Attorney General Letitia James leads a coalition challenging the legality of tariffs imposed under a never-before-used 1974 trade law.

top.title: Democrats flip 9th state seat since Trump took office
top.description: Democrat Alex Holladay won an Arkansas House special election Tuesday, extending a streak in which Republicans have flipped zero Democratic-held seats.

top.title: US and Venezuela probe secret Maduro-era oil deals
top.description: Dozens of confidential contracts let investors pump Venezuelan crude while hiding their identities to evade U.S. sanctions, according to Bloomberg.

top.title: China sets slowest defense budget growth since 2022 at 7%
top.description: The official military budget rises to 1.91 trillion yuan ($277 billion), even as the U.S. and allies accelerate their own spending.

top.title: Appeals court blocks Musk deposition in USAID lawsuit
top.description: A federal appeals court ruled 2-1 that Musk does not have to testify under oath about his role in dismantling the foreign aid agency while leading DOGE.

top.title: Venezuela to reform mining law after Burgum visit
top.description: Acting President Rodriguez said the legislation, which would open gold, diamonds and rare earths to foreign companies, will be submitted in days.

top.title: Noem defends record in two days of heated Hill testimony
top.description: The DHS secretary faced bipartisan criticism over Minneapolis shootings, FEMA delays, and a GOP senator's public call for her resignation.

top.title: Johnson says US 'not at war' with Iran, contradicting Trump
top.description: The House speaker's claim clashes with the president's and defense secretary's own use of the word "war" to describe Operation Epic Fury.

top.title: Iran conflict threatens to divert US air defense missiles from Ukraine
top.description: Analysts warn that roughly 600 PAC-3 interceptors produced annually cannot meet demand from both Gulf allies and Ukraine.

top.title: Rubio pledges full US support to Turkey after NATO downs Iranian missile
top.description: NATO intercepted a ballistic missile from Iran heading toward Turkish airspace, the first such defense of a member state since the conflict erupted.

top.title: Tech giants sign Trump pledge to fund their own data center power
top.description: Seven major tech companies agreed to build or buy all electricity needed for AI data centers and prevent costs from reaching consumers.

top.title: Iraq suffers nationwide blackout as war disrupts gas supplies
top.description: A fuel shortage at a Basra power plant caused a cascading grid failure, as the U.S.-Israel-Iran conflict chokes Iraq's Iranian gas imports.

top.title: House panel subpoenas AG Bondi over Epstein files
top.description: Five Republicans joined all Democrats on the Oversight Committee in a 24-19 vote compelling Bondi to testify on withheld Epstein documents.

top.title: DOJ proposes rule to let Bondi block state bar ethics probes into its lawyers
top.description: The regulation, published Wednesday in the Federal Register, would let the attorney general suspend state-level investigations while DOJ conducts its own review.

top.title: New study finds sea levels far higher than assumed, raising flood risk for millions
top.description: Lidar satellite data reveals coastal lands are much lower than radar-based models suggested, tripling the population estimated to be at risk.

top.title: Bondi lets DOJ appointees attend partisan events again
top.description: The attorney general revoked Biden-era rules that barred political appointees from campaign rallies, fundraisers, and other partisan activities.

top.title: European stocks post biggest gain since May on Iran diplomacy hopes
top.description: Markets rallied Wednesday after reports that Iran made indirect contact with the U.S. to negotiate an end to the conflict.

top.title: Talarico wins Texas Democratic Senate nod amid voting chaos
top.description: Hundreds of Dallas voters were turned away at polls after a GOP-driven shift to precinct-specific voting sparked court battles over extended hours.

top.title: Pentagon says it killed Iranian commander behind Trump assassination plot
top.description: The announcement came on day five of Operation Epic Fury, the U.S.-Israeli campaign that has already killed Iran's supreme leader.

top.title: SpaceX launches 600th Starlink satellite of 2026
top.description: The Falcon 9 mission from Cape Canaveral carried 29 satellites, while SpaceX eyes commercial Starship operations as soon as next year.

top.title: Cooper, Whatley win NC Senate primary showdown
top.description: The former Democratic governor and Trump-backed ex-RNC co-chair will face off in a race that could help decide Senate control.

top.title: Chinese projects in Iran face uncertainty amid US-Israeli strikes
top.description: Reuters found state-backed Chinese firms were pursuing energy, construction, and trade projects in Iran just weeks before the military campaign began.

top.title: Kuwaiti F/A-18 pilot fired 3 missiles that downed U.S. F-15s, reports say
top.description: Initial reports point to a single Kuwaiti fighter jet, not ground-based air defenses, as the source of the friendly fire incident that destroyed three American warplanes.

top.title: Hinojosa wins Texas Democratic primary, will face Abbott in November
top.description: The state representative captured over 60% of the vote, setting up what polls suggest could be Texas' most competitive governor's race in years.

top.title: Israel vows to assassinate Khamenei's successor as son Mojtaba reportedly chosen
top.description: Iran's Assembly of Experts named Mojtaba Khamenei as supreme leader under IRGC pressure, days after his father was killed in a U.S.-Israeli strike.

top.title: House panel seeks testimony from Gates, Black in Epstein probe
top.description: Commerce Secretary Lutnick also agreed to testify voluntarily, avoiding a potential subpoena over his ties to Jeffrey Epstein.

top.title: US readying draft indictment of Venezuela's interim leader Rodriguez
top.description: Federal prosecutors are allegedly preparing corruption and money laundering charges as leverage over Venezuela's interim president, according to Reuters.

top.title: Iran warns advanced weapons not yet deployed in conflict
top.description: Tehran's Defense Ministry claims it has reserved its most powerful systems, rejecting Trump's assertion that Iran's military has been neutralized.

top.title: Ship hit near UAE's Fujairah as Gulf attacks escalate
top.description: The latest strike is part of a wave of attacks on commercial shipping since U.S.-Israeli operations against Iran began on Feb. 28.

top.title: Poll finds half of Americans now support abolishing ICE
top.description: Support hit 50% for the first time in YouGov polling, with opposition falling to an all-time low of 39%, amid fallout from deadly shootings.

top.title: Merz meets Trump as Germany's military buildup reshapes Europe
top.description: Germany's chancellor visited the White House as Berlin's plan to more than double defense spending draws both applause and anxiety from European allies.

top.title: Survey finds one in three European women face violence
top.description: A sweeping new survey of more than 114,000 women across all 27 EU member states found that most cases of violence go unreported.

top.title: Syria deploys thousands of troops to Lebanon border
top.description: The buildup, which began in February and accelerated recently, aims to block Hezbollah infiltration, according to Reuters.

top.title: Dr. Oz opens fraud probe into New York's Medicaid program
top.description: The CMS administrator sent Gov. Hochul 50 questions about cost controls and provider oversight in the state's $124 billion program.

top.title: Sudan accuses Ethiopia of drone attacks in its civil war
top.description: Khartoum says drones launched from Ethiopian territory struck targets inside Sudan, the first time it has directly implicated its neighbor in the conflict.

top.title: Israel says it destroyed secret Iranian nuclear site near Tehran
top.description: The IDF said it struck a covert underground facility where scientists were developing nuclear weapons components, the first such site revealed since June 2025.

top.title: Lutnick volunteers to testify on Epstein ties
top.description: The commerce secretary's offer comes as lawmakers were preparing a bipartisan subpoena to compel his appearance before the House Oversight Committee.

top.title: Ukraine refuses EU requests to inspect damaged Druzhba pipeline
top.description: Kyiv denied access to the EU ambassador and top EU leaders, citing security concerns, as Hungary leverages the dispute to block a €90 billion loan.

top.title: FCC chair says Paramount-WBD merger will be approved 'quickly'
top.description: FCC Chairman Brendan Carr told CNBC the deal is "cleaner" than Netflix's earlier bid, signaling a smoother regulatory path for the $110 billion acquisition.

top.title: DOJ reverses itself, seeks to revive Trump law firm cases
top.description: Hours after moving to drop appeals of rulings that struck down Trump's executive orders targeting four law firms, the Justice Department reversed course.

top.title: IAEA confirms damage at Iran's Natanz nuclear site
top.description: The U.N. nuclear watchdog said satellite imagery shows entrance buildings were hit but no radiological consequences are expected.

top.title: Supreme Court lets California schools tell parents about child's gender identity
top.description: The 6-3 ruling reinstates a federal injunction blocking state laws that limited schools from disclosing students' transgender status to parents.

top.title: Noem refuses to retract 'terrorist' label for slain citizens
top.description: At a Senate hearing, the DHS secretary denied calling Alex Pretti a "domestic terrorist," saying she described the incident, not the person.

top.title: Ukraine says it recaptured more land than Russia seized in February
top.description: Kyiv's top general called it the first month Ukraine outpaced Russia in territorial gains since the 2024 Kursk incursion.

top.title: Supreme Court blocks redrawing of NYC's only GOP district
top.description: The ruling preserves Rep. Nicole Malliotakis's Staten Island-based seat for 2026, halting a state court order that found the map dilutes minority votes.

top.title: Russia's Medvedev warns of World War III over US strikes on Iran
top.description: The deputy chairman of Russia's Security Council called Trump's regime-change campaign "a grave mistake" that has put all Americans at risk.

top.title: Bolivia raids homes to recover cash looted from deadly plane crash
top.description: Security forces searched at least 22 homes near El Alto, where a military plane carrying $62 million in new banknotes crashed last week, killing 22.

top.title: Judge blocks ICE visit restrictions for all of Congress
top.description: A federal judge struck down DHS's seven-day notice requirement for the third time, now extending the ruling to protect all lawmakers, not just the 13 who sued.

top.title: Judge rules Trump bid to kill NYC congestion pricing unlawful
top.description: U.S. District Judge Lewis Liman found the Transportation Department's 2025 move to terminate Manhattan's first-in-the-nation tolling program exceeded its authority.

top.title: House panel releases Clinton's Epstein deposition videos
top.description: More than nine hours of footage show Bill and Hillary Clinton answering questions about Jeffrey Epstein after months of legal battles.

top.title: Rubio says Israel's strike plan triggered US attack on Iran
top.description: The secretary of state said the U.S. struck Iran preemptively after learning Israel would attack, fearing Tehran's automatic retaliation against American forces.

top.title: Treasury ends Anthropic AI use as agencies comply with Trump ban
top.description: Federal housing agencies also cut ties with the AI company, which refused to drop safety guardrails on surveillance and autonomous weapons for the Pentagon.

top.title: Sanders and Khanna introduce 5% wealth tax on billionaires
top.description: The bill would tax America's 938 billionaires to fund $3,000 direct payments to most households and expand social programs.

top.title: Navy intel chief warns China could strike US from its own waters by 2030s
top.description: New submarines armed with advanced ballistic missiles will be able to target large portions of the U.S. from Chinese coastal waters, Rear Adm. Brookes told Congress.

top.title: Rubio says US ground troops not needed in Iran
top.description: Secretary of State Rubio told lawmakers the campaign's goals can be met through air and naval power, but Trump hasn't ruled out ground forces.
